commands.review_summary:
The PR serializes Android Gateway event handling, accumulates realtime Talk transcript chunks, queues PCM playback writes, and adds focused Android tests plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: Partially: source inspection gives a high-confidence path for transcript deltas replacing prior text on current main. The frame-order and playback-choppiness part is source-plausible, but I did not establish a live Android device reproduction.
commands.review_followup: Human review should wait for contributor-supplied redacted real Android Talk proof; automation cannot create proof from the contributor's device/setup.

target.title: Fix Android realtime talk playback stability
target.body:
## Summary

- Serialize Android Gateway websocket frame handling so realtime talk events are applied in wire order instead of racing each other on separate coroutines.
- Accumulate realtime transcript delta chunks in the Android voice conversation instead of replacing the displayed assistant text with the latest fragment.
- Queue realtime PCM audio chunks through a single AudioTrack writer, write each chunk fully, and use a larger playback buffer to reduce response choppiness.
- Add focused Android unit coverage for transcript accumulation and ordered Gateway event dispatch, plus an Unreleased changelog entry.

## Root Cause

The Android Gateway websocket listener launched a new coroutine for every incoming text frame. Realtime talk transcript and audio events could therefore be handled concurrently instead of in the order received from the relay.

In the Android voice UI, transcript events were treated as complete snapshots and trimmed before being written to the active conversation row. When the relay sent delta-shaped chunks, the app replaced the row with only the latest piece, which matches the reported “last piece only” display.

Realtime PCM chunks were written directly from the event handler to AudioTrack. That made playback sensitive to frame-handler scheduling, partial writes, and a small per-chunk stream buffer, all of which can produce choppy assistant responses.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Android realtime Talk voice UI transcript deltas were shown as the latest fragment only; realtime PCM assistant audio playback could be choppy from unordered Gateway event handling and direct partial AudioTrack writes.

Real environment tested: Local Android unit-test and ktlint environment in temp worktree `/tmp/openclaw-android-realtime-talk` with Android SDK `/home/vac/android-sdk`. Gateway logs reviewed for `VAC-S24U`, `samsung SM-S928U1`, Android app `2026.5.19-dev`, on May 19, 2026.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `ANDROID_HOME=/home/vac/android-sdk ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests 'ai.openclaw.app.voice.TalkModeManagerTest' --tests 'ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionInvokeTest'`; `ANDROID_HOME=/home/vac/android-sdk ./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: Targeted Android unit tests passed; ktlint passed; diff check passed. New tests cover realtime transcript delta accumulation, final delta completion, and ordered Gateway event dispatch.

Observed result after fix: Android voice conversation state now accumulates realtime transcript chunks into the active assistant row; Gateway websocket frames are dispatched serially; realtime audio chunks are queued and written fully through AudioTrack with a larger stream buffer.

What was not tested: I did not run a live S24U realtime Talk session or listen to the voice on hardware because `adb`/logcat was unavailable in this environment and no live device proof was available. I also did not inspect the Telegram screenshot directly, so the screenshot itself is not independent proof in this PR.

## Verification

- PASS: `ANDROID_HOME=/home/vac/android-sdk ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests 'ai.openclaw.app.voice.TalkModeManagerTest' --tests 'ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionInvokeTest'`
- PASS: `ANDROID_HOME=/home/vac/android-sdk ./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck`
- PASS: `git diff --check`
- BLOCKED/ENV: `./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest ...` from the repo root failed because the Android wrapper lives under `apps/android` and the debug unit-test task is flavor-ambiguous.
- BLOCKED/ENV: An initial Android test attempt without `ANDROID_HOME=/home/vac/android-sdk` failed because the Android SDK path was not exported in the temp shell.
target.author: vacinc
